Category: Help Desk & IT Support (Information & Communication Technology)
Ad Text:
The role

We’re hiring an experienced generalist to support and improve client environments end-to-end: networks, M365, security, automation, the lot. You’ll work across a varied client base from our South Melbourne office, on-site with clients regularly, and have genuine input into how we build and run our internal tooling.

 

About Syntax

We’re a Melbourne MSP serving clients from 5 to 150 seats across numerous states and industries including but not limited to logistics, finance, real estate, and manufacturing.

Some are small offices. Some turn over $80M a year. We handle cloud, networking, M365, automation, VoIP, physical security, compliance, and OT, whatever the client actually needs to work properly.




What we’ve built, and why it matters for this role

Over the last couple of years we’ve built a lot of our own tools. There are countless automations running quieter parts of the business, SIEM triage automation, and various levels of AI autonomy across both internal and external systems.

Not because we were trying to be clever; because off-the-shelf didn’t fit.

This matters for two reasons. First, the work is more varied than most MSP roles.

On any given week you might tune a firewall rule, debug an automation workflow reconciling vendor invoices, investigate a SIEM alert, walk a new client through their compliance posture, troubleshoot a dodgy switch at a warehouse, and explain to a director why their phone system and their MFA are part of the same conversation.

 

 

What we’d see you growing into

Support and improve client environments end-to-end, not just close tickets.

Spot the connections between systems. If a user’s Outlook is slow, you’re thinking DNS, ISP, recent changes, MFA, TLS, M365 service health, not just restarting the client.

Work on projects: migrations, network redesigns, security rollouts, automation builds.

Contribute to internal tooling. You don’t need to be a developer, but curiosity about how our automation and dashboards work will take you a long way here.

Be on-site with clients regularly. Server rooms and boardrooms in the same day, sometimes.




What the first year looks like, honestly

The first few months involve more systems, vendors, and client contexts than you’ll feel comfortable with.

Everyone who joins an MSP feels this; we’ve just got a wider stack than most. We’ll give you context, documentation where it exists, and real support; but we’ll also expect you to dig in, ask sharp questions, and start connecting dots on your own.

Beyond that, there’s genuine room to specialise (automation and integration, security, consulting, technical leadership), depending on where your interests land.

 

What we’re looking for

3+ years of varied IT experience. MSP background ideal, or a generalist in-house role where you wore many hats.

A different thinker. We want you because you aren’t just a MSP Tech.

Strong generalist fundamentals. Networks, Windows, M365, identity, backups, storage. Bonus if you’ve got depth in firewalls, automation, or security tooling.

Self Autonomy. You can identify what needs doing without being told each morning.

Client presence. You don’t need a suit, but you do need to be comfortable representing us in client environments.

Driver’s licence and Right to work in Australia.




Come ready to talk about a couple of messy problems you’ve untangled, a mistake you made along the way, and what you’d do differently now.

 

What you’d get

A competitive base with profit share once you’re established. We share the upside when the business does well.

Real variety and genuine input into how the business evolves.

A small, capable team. No politics, no layers. If you’ve got a better way, we’ll try it.

Futurism, modern and traditional IT side by side. Racking gear at a DC one day, tuning a voice agent the next.

Flexibility, trust, and a human-first approach to how we work.

Job Title: Technical Practice Lead (MSP) - Microsoft & AI Platforms
Ad Text:
Ready to set the technical standard and make sure work is done right the first time, every time?

We’re looking for a Technical Practice Lead to act as the technical authority and steward of engineering quality across our BAU operations for managed services, including the adoption of automation and AI capabilities in our technical processes.

If you have deep Microsoft technical expertise and a track record of setting and governing standards and translating that into real-world operational improvements - without owning ticket queues - this role is for you!

About the Role

You’ll define and uphold the technical standards that ensure our managed services are delivered consistently, sustainably and with confidence.

You will also shape how we adopt automation and AI within our support processes in a practical, outcome-drive way.

This is a senior role focused on technical judgement and long‑term quality — not day‑to‑day operational response.

Your impact will be seen in scalable service quality, reduced reactivity, increased automation and AI adoption and predictable outcomes for our customers.

What You’ll Be Doing

Technical Authority & Standards

Define, maintain, and enforce technical standards across BAU operations
Approve or reject non‑standard technical approaches and exceptions
Act as the final authority on architectural and platform‑level decisions in BAU contexts
Ensure consistent technical decision‑making across L2 and L3 engineers
Assess and report on adherence to agreed standards
Prioritise and oversee initiatives to reduce customer technical debts

Reduction of Repeat & Systemic Incidents

Identify recurring failure patterns and systemic technical issues
Drive high‑quality root cause analysis for major and systemic incidents
Ensure corrective actions are durable and embedded — not just documented
Actively measure and reduce repeat incidents over time

Escalation Quality & Decision Depth

Improve the quality and clarity of escalations reaching senior engineers
Coach L2 teams on diagnostic rigour and decision‑making within standards
Act as a technical backstop for complex judgement, not a default escalation point

Capability Uplift & Technical Maturity

Uplift L2 and L3 capability through guidance, patterns, and structured review
Ensure incident learnings translate into better runbooks, standards and automation opportunities
Provide leadership with systemic insights rather than ticket‑level noise

AI & Automation Enablement

Identify and prioritise opportunities to reduce manual effort through AI and automation
Embed AI capability into standards, runbooks and engineering practices 

Pre-sales and Solution Design Contribution

Define standard solution patterns used in proposals and RFP responses
Review and approve technical designs to ensure they are deliverable and aligned with standards
Contribute to high-value opportunities by shaping architecture, security and AI/Copilot positioning


About You

You’ll have proven experience defining and embedding technical standards across Microsoft‑based environments.

You’ve demonstrated sound technical judgement across platforms such as Microsoft 365, Azure, identity, security, and core infrastructure — and you’ve used that experience to drive consistency through standards, reduce rework, and improve long‑term stability.

You’re comfortable influencing how decisions are made and how problems are solved, ensuring those decisions are applied consistently in delivery without needing to own day‑to‑day operational flow.

Professionally, you will have:

Ten+ years of experience working as a senior engineer/architect/technical manager preferably at an MSP

Technically, you will have extensive experience and consider yourself an expert in the design, implementation and management of technologies including:

Mandatory Skills/Experience

Microsoft 365 including Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint
Entra ID
Microsoft 365 security (Defender for Endpoints, Cloud, DLP, CA policies)
Microsoft Azure
Microsoft Intune including Autopilot
Networking (switching, WiFi and Firewalls from Sophos, Dell, Cisco or Ubiquiti)

Desirable Skills/Experience


Experience with Copilot, AI tooling or automation within Microsoft environments 
Copilot Studio / Copilot Agents
Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD)
Cyber security tools and frameworks
Windows Server
Server hardware, storage and virtualisation (eg, Dell, VMWare, HyperV etc)

About VISITS

We’re a national Managed IT and Cyber Security Services Provider with our head office on St Kilda Road, Melbourne. Our clients are mid-sized to small organisations – including listed companies, professional services firms and NFPs – who want more than just IT support.

They stay with us because we deliver exceptional business outcomes and standout customer experiences. And we’re growing fast!

Perks and benefits

We value our people and offer competitive salaries, strong employee benefits, and genuine opportunities for career growth. Every team member receives a personalised training and professional development plan to support ongoing progression.

Our Melbourne head office on St Kilda Road is a great place to work, with breakfast, snacks and light lunches provided, a café‑quality coffee machine and easy access to public transport. We also enjoy a strong team culture with regular social events, including Friday night drinks.

You’ll be based in our Melbourne office, with flexibility to work from home.

Additional benefits include:

Novated leasing, including FBT‑free electric vehicle options (where eligible)
IT equipment and home NBN at cost
Access to company cars
Optional involvement in fundraising initiatives

Employment Specifics

This is a full-time, permanent role.

You must have a full driver’s licence, must be an Australian Citizen (or have ongoing work rights in Australia) and your employment is subject to completing a Criminal History Clearance Check.

Remuneration will be negotiated based on your experience and qualifications.

Bring your judgement. Set the standard. Apply now.

Direct applications only - we are not engaging recruitment agencies for this role.

Please note that we will only be contacting applicants that have been shortlisted.
